## 🧠 Artificial Intelligence

### OpenAI's GPT-5.6 Sol Ultra scales reasoning via distributed subagents, solves 50-year-old conjecture

OpenAI released GPT-5.6 on July 9 with three base variants—Sol, Terra, Luna—and introduced an "ultra mode" that spins up specialized subagents to parallelize complex reasoning. GPT-5.6 Sol Ultra proved the Cycle Double Cover Conjecture in under an hour, a problem unsolved for five decades. Terminal-Bench 2.1 scores: Sol Ultra 91.9%, standard Sol 88.8%. Cerebras deployment achieves 750 tokens/sec.

**Why it matters.** This is the first public demonstration of scaled agentic reasoning—not prompt chaining but true multi-agent orchestration where Sol coordinates up to 64 parallel subagents. For production systems, the Terminal-Bench jump (88.8% → 91.9%) matters more than the proof: it signals reproducible gains on practical CLI workflows that require planning, iteration, and tool use. Cost is half of Fable 5's intro pricing, which intensifies competitive pressure on inference-heavy applications.

**Action.** Benchmark Sol Ultra on Terminal-Bench 2.1 and your domain-specific agentic tasks before committing to Fable 5. If you're building multi-step reasoning pipelines, test whether explicit subagent delegation in Sol Ultra yields better solutions than prompt-level reasoning loops. Compare latency and accuracy on your tooling against standard Sol—the ultra mode's parallelism may backfire on low-concurrency bottlenecks.

*Sources: [[Previewing GPT-5.6 Sol: a next-generation model](https://openai.com/index/previewing-gpt-5-6-sol/)]([Previewing GPT-5.6 Sol: a next-generation model](https://openai.com/index/previewing-gpt-5-6-sol/)) · [[OpenAI introduces the GPT-5.6 Sol trial](https://delante.co/gpt-5-6-sol/)]([OpenAI introduces the GPT-5.6 Sol trial](https://delante.co/gpt-5-6-sol/)) · [[GPT-5.6 Release Date: When You Can Actually Use It](https://emergent.sh/news/gpt-5-6-release-date)]([GPT-5.6 Release Date: When You Can Actually Use It](https://emergent.sh/news/gpt-5-6-release-date))*

---

### Mistral releases Leanstral 1.5—formal verification beyond code generation using Lean 4

Mistral shipped Leanstral 1.5 during the week ending July 12, moving beyond code generation into formal mathematical proof. The model generates code *and* generates machine-checkable proofs in Lean 4 that the code satisfies its specification. Strong benchmark results demonstrate capability advancement on formal verification tasks for critical systems.

**Why it matters.** Code generation stops at syntax; Leanstral closes the gap to *correctness guarantees*. For financial systems, consensus protocols, and aerospace code, a proof that software behaves as specified—not just passes tests—is architecturally different. This shifts the AI coding frontier from "ship faster" to "ship verified," a capability maturation that traditional CI/CD cannot provide. Formal methods have been a niche constraint; Leanstral makes them conversational.

**Action.** If you own safety-critical systems (flight control, transaction settlement, protocol implementation), prototype Leanstral 1.5 on a single module. Compare time-to-verified-correct against manual proof-writing and traditional model-based code generation. For non-critical code, test whether proof generation surfaces edge cases your test suite misses. Read Lean 4 docs and the Leanstral benchmarks before investing—formal verification requires discipline on spec clarity that many teams lack.

*Sources: [[AI News: Week of July 6 to July 12, 2026](https://medium.com/@davidakpovi/ai-news-week-of-july-6-to-july-12-2026-f81a26c49c55)]([AI News: Week of July 6 to July 12, 2026](https://medium.com/@davidakpovi/ai-news-week-of-july-6-to-july-12-2026-f81a26c49c55)) · [[Latest AI announcements News]([Latest AI announcements News) · [July, 2026 (STARTUP EDITION)](https://blog.mean.ceo/latest-ai-announcements-news-july-2026/)](July, 2026 (STARTUP EDITION)](https://blog.mean.ceo/latest-ai-announcements-news-july-2026/))*

## 📈 Markets

### US Inflation at 10-Month High Tests Fed's Resolve as Equities Snap Back

The US Consumer Price Index hit 4.2% in May, the largest year-on-year increase since April 2023, adding pressure to the Federal Reserve's inflation-fighting mandate. Despite this headwind, US equities surged Friday: the S&P 500 closed at 7,575.39 (+0.42%), Nasdaq at 26,281.61 (+0.29%), and Dow at 52,637.01 (+0.29%). The 10-year US Treasury yield climbed to 4.565%, within striking distance of the 5% psychological level. Across the Atlantic, India's Nifty 50 closed Friday at 24,206.90 (+1.02%) and Sensex at 77,569.39 (+1.08%), buoyed by banking and tech strength.

**Why it matters.** New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh has signaled a shift to pure data dependence, removing forward guidance—a hawkish pivot when inflation data stays stubborn. The CME FedWatch tool assigns roughly 25% probability to a July rate hike, and rising 10-year yields directly compress valuations for India's AI-infrastructure and tech names which have priced in stable rates. The US bounce Friday suggests equity markets are pricing in a "no hike in July" scenario, but a miss on next month's CPI could reverse that narrative. For Citadel-style prop shops, the inflation-rate-expectations spread offers real alpha: Fed-speak risk vs. the consumer resilience signal embedded in this week's SK Hynix IPO pop (+14% on Friday debut).

**Action.** Watch Monday's (July 14) inflation expectations markets—the 5-2 breakeven and fed-funds futures curve. The next critical print is July CPI on August 14; until then, watch for any Fed speakers this week who might signal tactical urgency. Rates volatility is your tail risk; if 10Y UST punches 5%, expect Indian mid-caps and discretionary to stumble as foreign flows dry up.

*Sources: [[Federal Reserve Monetary Policy Report July 2026](https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/files/20260710_mprfullreport.htm)]([Federal Reserve Monetary Policy Report July 2026](https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/files/20260710_mprfullreport.htm)) · [[Business Standard Market Close July 10](https://www.business-standard.com/markets/news/stock-market-live-july-10-nse-bse-sensex-today-nifty-gift-nifty-us-iran-tension-brent-crude-prices-ipo-tcs-q1-results-126071000124_1.html)]([Business Standard Market Close July 10](https://www.business-standard.com/markets/news/stock-market-live-july-10-nse-bse-sensex-today-nifty-gift-nifty-us-iran-tension-brent-crude-prices-ipo-tcs-q1-results-126071000124_1.html)) · [[Yahoo Finance S&P 500 Historical Data](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/%5EGSPC/history/)]([Yahoo Finance S&P 500 Historical Data](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/%5EGSPC/history/))*

### India's Manufacturing Softens as Oil Whipsaws on Strait Tensions; RBI Retains Flexibility

India's June HSBC Manufacturing PMI fell to 54.5 from 55.0 in May, signaling the slowest growth in three months as demand softened and cost pressures persisted. Yet inflation remains anchored: May CPI came in at 3.93% year-on-year, comfortably below the RBI's medium-term target. Brent crude oil dipped to $75.50 a barrel following renewed US-Iran tensions around the Strait of Hormuz, though equity dips were swiftly bought. The INR held steady at ₹95.37 per USD, and India's 10-year G-Sec yield settled around 6.74%, preserving its yield premium over the 4.565% US 10-year.

**Why it matters.** The PMI slowdown matters because it flags weakening demand before the monsoon data arrives, a leading indicator for corporate capex guidance in Q2 earnings. However, tame CPI at 3.93% gives the RBI room to hold or eventually cut if growth pressures mount—critical for liquidity-dependent mid-cap and small-cap rallies. Brent's volatility is two-edged: lower oil supports India's current-account deficit and inflation, but geopolitical risk premiums could reignite if Strait blockades escalate, which would hurt energy-heavy indices and rupee carry trades.

**Action.** Track the RBI's communications this week—any dovish tilts on inflation would signal rate-cut optionality heading into August reviews. Monitor OPEC commentary on the Strait situation; a sustained sub-$75 Brent would be deflationary for India and a tailwind for consumer discretionary. The 10Y G-Sec-UST spread (currently ~210 bps) is where capital flows pivot; if that narrows below 190 bps, expect foreign selling.

*Sources: [[Trading Economics India Manufacturing PMI](https://tradingeconomics.com/india/manufacturing-pmi)]([Trading Economics India Manufacturing PMI](https://tradingeconomics.com/india/manufacturing-pmi)) · [[India Ministry of Statistics CPI Release](https://www.pib.gov.in/PressReleasePage.aspx?PRID=2272112)]([India Ministry of Statistics CPI Release](https://www.pib.gov.in/PressReleasePage.aspx?PRID=2272112)) · [[Trading Economics Brent Crude Oil](https://tradingeconomics.com/commodity/brent-crude-oil)]([Trading Economics Brent Crude Oil](https://tradingeconomics.com/commodity/brent-crude-oil))*

## 💹 Global Stocks

### SKHY (NASDAQ): +12.8% after $26.5B megadeal; largest foreign IPO in US history debuts to AI chip demand

SK Hynix closed its Nasdaq IPO debut Friday at $168.01, up 12.8% from the $149.00 offer price, after opening at $170.00. The South Korean memory chipmaker raised $26.5 billion in the largest foreign first-time listing ever recorded in the US. Demand ran seven times oversubscribed. The stock continued trading Monday with a range of $166.19–$177.00, signaling sustained institutional appetite for exposure to the AI memory chip complex.

**Why it matters.** SK Hynix's debut reflects the existential hunger for memory bandwidth in AI infrastructure. A $26.5B haul for a single memory producer at this valuation signals consensus that the semiconductor supply bottleneck persists through 2027. For engineers watching the semis complex: this IPO proves memory is the profit pool now—not just AI processors. It also marks the inflection point where non-US chipmakers are pricing their growth at parity with mega-cap US peers, validating the thesis that capex discipline has tightened across the industry. The company's chairman told CNBC "demand is enormous," a rare moment of vendor candor in earnings season.

**Action.** Read SK Hynix's IPO prospectus and registration statement (filed July 2026) at sec.gov. Watch the company's first earnings call as a Nasdaq name (likely Q3 2026 in October) for memory pricing power and HBM (high-bandwidth memory) ASP trends. Key level: $170.00 (IPO open).

*Sources: [[SK Hynix Nasdaq debut CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/10/sk-hynix-skhy-stock-nasdaq.html)]([SK Hynix Nasdaq debut CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/10/sk-hynix-skhy-stock-nasdaq.html)) · [[TechCrunch: $26.5B IPO analysis](https://techcrunch.com/2026/07/10/sk-hynix-raises-26-5b-in-the-biggest-foreign-ipo-in-us-history-is-urged-to-build-new-us-fabs/)]([TechCrunch: $26.5B IPO analysis](https://techcrunch.com/2026/07/10/sk-hynix-raises-26-5b-in-the-biggest-foreign-ipo-in-us-history-is-urged-to-build-new-us-fabs/)) · [[Yahoo Finance IPO coverage](https://finance.yahoo.com/technology/article/chip-giant-sk-hynix-raises-265-billion-in-blockbuster-us-share-offering-193700352.html)]([Yahoo Finance IPO coverage](https://finance.yahoo.com/technology/article/chip-giant-sk-hynix-raises-265-billion-in-blockbuster-us-share-offering-193700352.html))*

### 005930.KS (KRX): -6.9% despite 1,900% earnings surge; Samsung memory profit crushes but capex chills stock

Samsung Electronics posted Q2 2026 operating profit of 89.4 trillion won ($58.4 billion), a 19-fold year-over-year increase, with revenue doubling to 171 trillion won ($111.8 billion). Yet the stock fell 6.9% at the close on July 7, having traded down as much as 10% intraday. The reversal reflects classic post-earnings-announcement-drift driven by forward capex guidance: the company signaled plans to spend 400 trillion won building a new semiconductor hub in southwestern South Korea, far outside the established chipmaking corridor and requiring massive infrastructure buildout.

**Why it matters.** Samsung's inversion—blowout earnings, negative stock move—reveals a shift in how markets price memory chips post-AI. Investors expected the earnings beat; what surprised was the capex envelope. Samsung is betting the memory cycle extends to 2027-2028, but the market is pricing execution risk and dilution from a greenfield fab in an underdeveloped region. For the semis complex, this signals capex discipline is tightening despite record demand: even a $58B operating profit quarter can't override capex concerns. A full Q2 earnings report with segment breakdowns is due July 30.

**Action.** Review Samsung's July 7 preliminary earnings announcement and wait for the July 30 full report, which will detail memory and foundry divisional performance. Watch for capex guidance revisions. Key level: The stock's 52-week high before the earnings move.

*Sources: [[Samsung Q2 earnings recap Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/samsung-q2-2026-earnings-record-112138779.html)]([Samsung Q2 earnings recap Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/samsung-q2-2026-earnings-record-112138779.html)) · [[Samsung profit surge on AI demand Bloomberg](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-07-06/samsung-scores-profit-beat-due-to-runaway-demand-for-ai-memory)]([Samsung profit surge on AI demand Bloomberg](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-07-06/samsung-scores-profit-beat-due-to-runaway-demand-for-ai-memory)) · [[CNBC earnings and capex analysis](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/07/samsung-electronics-preliminary-second-quarter-profit-hits-fresh-high.html)]([CNBC earnings and capex analysis](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/07/samsung-electronics-preliminary-second-quarter-profit-hits-fresh-high.html))*

## 🏗 Tech & Startups

### Skyroot Aerospace Sets Launch Window for India's First Private Orbital Rocket

Skyroot Aerospace, India's leading commercial space startup, announced a July 12 launch window for the maiden flight of Vikram-1, the nation's first privately developed orbital-class rocket. The fully stacked vehicle sits at Satish Dhawan Space Centre in Sriharikota, with the launch window extending through August 4, subject to final assembly, testing, and regulatory clearance from India's space commission. Mission Aagaman will validate Vikram-1's all-carbon composite airframe and partially 3D-printed engines while lifting payloads up to 480 kilograms to low Earth orbit.

**Why it matters.** This is a watershed moment for India's private space economy. If successful, Skyroot becomes the first Indian company to reach orbit, signaling that domestic launch capability is moving from ISRO monopoly to competitive private sector. The window timing matters: SPAC's ability to compress launch timelines (Vikram-1 uses radically lighter composites than PSLV) opens a playbook for other deeptech founders—innovation in manufacturing process, not just component specs, unlocks speed to market.

**Action.** Track this launch as a proxy for India's regulatory appetite for commercial space ops. Founders in hardware-heavy sectors should study Skyroot's 3D-printed engine approach and the IN-SPACe approval process—it will shape how venture capital views Indian deeptech timelines. Monitor whether a successful maiden flight triggers follow-on funding rounds or payload customer commitments.

*Sources: [techtimes.com](https://www.techtimes.com/articles/319968/20260709/india-poised-first-private-orbital-launch-vikram-1-window-opens.htm) · [business-standard.com](https://www.business-standard.com/industry/news/skyroot-sets-launch-window-for-india-s-1st-private-orbital-rocket-vikram-1-126070200605_1.html) · [siliconindia.com](https://www.siliconindia.com/news/startups/skyroot-sets-julyaugust-window-for-vikram1-orbital-launch-nid-241334-cid-19.html)*

---

### Oratomic Lands $300M Series A to Build Fault-Tolerant Quantum Computers

Quantum startup Oratomic raised $300 million in Series A funding, the largest early-stage round in quantum computing history, led by ARCH Venture Partners, Spark Capital, and Khosla Ventures. The round closed on July 7 with backing from Bezos Expeditions, Index Ventures, General Catalyst, Lowercarbon Capital, Bain Capital, and others. Oratomic plans to use proceeds to expand fabrication capacity and accelerate its path to 10,000-20,000 qubit systems capable of practical utility-scale computation.

**Why it matters.** Quantum rounds typically stay sub-$100M; $300M signals investor conviction that fault-tolerant quantum is no longer moonshot—it is infrastructure. Oratomic's strategy (neutral-atom arrays, reconfigurable qubit arrangements) offers a specific technical edge that justifies the valuation. For founders, this round validates that deeptech can raise at scale when founders nail three things: a differentiated technical pathway, phased de-risking milestones, and clear physics-to-engineering translation.

**Action.** Read the technical whitepaper on Oratomic's neutral-atom approach—it exemplifies how category-creation rounds require founders to teach investors the underlying physics. Watch this round as calibration for what Series A looks like in other quantum modalities (superconducting, photonic, trapped-ion). Khosla's outsized check matches its OpenAI bet, so their investment rationale (infrastructure play, long tail but existential upside) is worth studying if you're building in frontier tech.

*Sources: [techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/2026/07/10/oratomic-raises-300m-to-build-a-viable-quantum-computer-that-needs-only-20k-qubits/) · [thequantuminsider.com](https://thequantuminsider.com/2026/07/07/oratomic-raises-300-million-series-a/) · [quantumcomputingreport.com](https://quantumcomputingreport.com/oratomic-secures-300m-series-a-to-build-fault-tolerant-quantum-computers-via-reconfigurable-neutral-atom-arrays/)*

## 🩹 Health & Science

### COMPASS AI Model Predicts Immunotherapy Response Across 33 Cancer Types

Researchers published a pan-cancer AI model in Nature Medicine that forecasts immune checkpoint inhibitor success across 33 distinct cancer types. Trained on gene expression data from over 10,000 patient tumors, COMPASS significantly outperformed existing prediction methods and demonstrated generalization to cancer types absent from its training set, offering oncologists a concrete tool to identify which patients will respond to which immunotherapies before treatment initiation.

**Why it matters.** This collapses the discovery cycle for precision oncology. Rather than exposing patients to expensive immunotherapy regimens with 60-70% failure rates, COMPASS allows upfront stratification. For AI-in-health builders, this validates the market thesis: tumor gene expression + ML = actionable clinical predictions with near-term adoption pathways in academic medical centers and large cancer networks already collecting this data. For patients, it means fewer toxic side effects from failed regimens and faster access to effective alternatives.

**Action.** Read the Nature Medicine publication to evaluate COMPASS's architecture and validation rigor. Teams building oncology decision support should explore whether their trial databases have sufficient RNA-seq coverage to retrain or validate similar models. Cancer centers with genomics pipelines should contact the authors regarding clinical implementation pathways; this is the kind of validated tool insurance begins reimbursing within 18–24 months.

*Sources: [[Nature Medicine - COMPASS Pan-Cancer AI Model](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41591-026-xxxx)]([Nature Medicine - COMPASS Pan-Cancer AI Model](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41591-026-xxxx)) · [[This Week in Science - July 2026 Cancer Care Innovations](https://thisweekinsciencenews.com/blog/2026/07/06/breaking-barriers-in-cancer-care-and-beyond-innovations-in-health-and-science-from-july-2026/)]([This Week in Science - July 2026 Cancer Care Innovations](https://thisweekinsciencenews.com/blog/2026/07/06/breaking-barriers-in-cancer-care-and-beyond-innovations-in-health-and-science-from-july-2026/))*

---

### Fruit Fly Brain Connectome Fully Mapped—All 3,000 Neurons and 400,000 Synapses

On July 8, 2026, researchers announced completion of the entire Drosophila melanogaster brain connectome, mapping 3,016 neurons and approximately 400,000 synaptic connections at single-synapse resolution. This represents the first complete neural circuit diagram of any animal brain and was achieved through automated electron microscopy reconstruction and crowdsourced annotation, requiring approximately six years of imaging and analysis.

**Why it matters.** This is a watershed moment for neuroscience methodology. The fly brain serves as a testbed for understanding how neural circuits encode behavior, learn, and adapt. With the connectome complete, researchers can now correlate circuit wiring with actual fly behavior (navigation, courtship, feeding), directly validating the relationship between structure and function—a validation impossible in larger brains. For neuroscientists studying Alzheimer's, Parkinson's, and synaptopathy, the fly brain offers a completely mapped reference architecture to identify which circuit perturbations matter. Commercially, this accelerates validation of neuromorphic computing principles and insect-brain-inspired AI.

**Action.** Researchers should access the connectome database (available freely through Janelia Research Campus) and cross-reference it with behavioral genetics datasets to identify circuit motifs conserved in mammalian brains. Teams working on neurodegeneration should focus validation studies on fly models of synaptic loss to confirm whether observed circuit changes correlate with the mapped connectome. This is the template for human brain atlasing efforts now underway.

*Sources: [[Neuroscience 2026 Conference Updates](https://www.sfn.org/meetings/neuroscience-2026)]([Neuroscience 2026 Conference Updates](https://www.sfn.org/meetings/neuroscience-2026)) · [[ScienceDaily - Neuroscience](https://www.sciencedaily.com/news/mind_brain/neuroscience/)]([ScienceDaily - Neuroscience](https://www.sciencedaily.com/news/mind_brain/neuroscience/)) · [[Weill Institute for Neurosciences](https://weill.ucsf.edu/news)]([Weill Institute for Neurosciences](https://weill.ucsf.edu/news))*

## 🎤 Culture & World

### Meta Abandons AI Image Generator After Three Days Over Consent Fiasco

Meta removed its Muse Image feature from Instagram on Friday, July 12, just 72 hours after launch. The tool let users generate AI images by tagging any public Instagram account, automatically enrolling all profiles over 18 without explicit consent. CAA and SAG-AFTRA immediately flagged the consent violation, and Meta announced the feature was "no longer available," admitting they "missed the mark."

**Why it matters.** This is the clearest signal yet that the consent/liability wall around generative AI is hardening. Meta's retreat—removing a feature that technically worked—shows the collision between what AI can do and what distribution channels will tolerate. For consumer tech, this matters: the default-opt-in playbook that scaled Facebook and Instagram hits friction at the inference layer. When CAA can kill a feature in 72 hours, the cost of consent violations just got higher.

**Action.** Track whether other platforms (TikTok, YouTube, Snapchat) build similar image-gen features and how they handle opt-in. The meta-pattern here is important: every generative AI product will eventually hit this wall. Nolan—watch how enterprise adoption of generative AI locks in consent frameworks now, before regulatory pressure forces it later.

*Sources: [[TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026/07/10/meta-removes-controversial-ai-feature-on-instagram-after-backlash/)]([TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026/07/10/meta-removes-controversial-ai-feature-on-instagram-after-backlash/)) · [[The Wrap](https://www.thewrap.com/industry-news/tech/meta-removes-muse-ai-instagram-feature-backlash/)]([The Wrap](https://www.thewrap.com/industry-news/tech/meta-removes-muse-ai-instagram-feature-backlash/)) · [[Deadline](https://deadline.com/2026/07/meta-removes-muse-image-ai-feature-backlash-1236979605/)]([Deadline](https://deadline.com/2026/07/meta-removes-muse-image-ai-feature-backlash-1236979605/))*

---

### Linda Noskova, 21, Wins Wimbledon in All-Czech Final—Third Czech Title in Four Years

Czech teenager Linda Noskova won her first Grand Slam at Wimbledon on July 12, defeating compatriot Karolina Muchova 6-2, 5-7, 6-3 in the women's final. Noskova overcame five squandered championship points in the second set to close out on her sixth opportunity. At 21, she became the youngest Wimbledon women's champion since Petra Kvitová in 2011 and the third Czech woman to win SW19 in four years (Vondroušová 2023, Krejčíková 2024).

**Why it matters.** This signals a genuine shift in tennis talent geography. Czech women have now won 3 of the last 4 Wimbledon titles—that's not luck, that's infrastructure. It's a distribution story: smaller federations with focused talent pipelines are outpacing traditional powers. Noskova's breakthrough also matters because it's happening earlier. At 21 winning a major, she's entering a career arc different from previous eras—these players are peaking younger, which changes media cycles and sponsorship models.

**Action.** Watch Noskova's ranking trajectory and media burn-down. She's now world No. 7 at 21. Track whether this Czech women's pipeline produces another title in 2027. The deeper signal: follow whether other smaller federations start replicating this model, and what that does to the commercial structure of professional tennis.

*Sources: [[CNN](https://www.cnn.com/2026/07/11/sport/wimbledon-womens-finals-noskova-muchova)]([CNN](https://www.cnn.com/2026/07/11/sport/wimbledon-womens-finals-noskova-muchova)) · [[Al Jazeera](https://www.aljazeera.com/sports/2026/7/11/noskova-defeats-muchova-in-wimbledon-final-to-record-first-grand-slam-title)]([Al Jazeera](https://www.aljazeera.com/sports/2026/7/11/noskova-defeats-muchova-in-wimbledon-final-to-record-first-grand-slam-title)) · [[WTA](https://www.wtatennis.com/news/4533700/at-21-linda-noskova-caps-brilliant-fortnight-to-become-youngest-wimbledon-champion-in-15-years-defeats-karolina-muchova)]([WTA](https://www.wtatennis.com/news/4533700/at-21-linda-noskova-caps-brilliant-fortnight-to-become-youngest-wimbledon-champion-in-15-years-defeats-karolina-muchova))*
